purpose: For every architectural asset: who may CREATE it, who may MUTATE it, who may READ it, who owns its LIFECYCLE. Answers 'if I change X, who breaks?' without re-reading the tree.

provenance.method: Synthesis of the Cycle-2 AST writer census (mutation_writers.json, transitions.json) + the Cycle-5 AST dependency/lock/txn census. Every ownership claim is backed by a writer site; a claim of SOLE ownership is backed by an AST writer census, never a grep (Cycle-2 method note).
provenance.confidence_scale: CERTAIN = AST-verified writer set. HIGH = source-read, single-site. UNKNOWN = recorded in unknowns.json.

ownership_semantics.authoritative_owner: the single module whose contract defines the asset's legal values and transitions
ownership_semantics.mutation_authority: every module that WRITES it (AST writer census)
ownership_semantics.read_authority: every module that READS it (informative; not exhaustive where readers are many)
ownership_semantics.lifecycle_authority: who CREATES and who RETIRES/DELETES it

persistent_entities.lifecycle_authority: ingest creates (content-addressed, setdefault -> replay is a no-op); ledger.retire_source is the ONLY retirement path (operator-only; the FILE is left on disk)
moments.ingest_moments creates; adjust.retire is the ONLY irreversible retirement (ledger.reconcile_moments REFUSES to un-retire, ledger.py:636-642)
persistent_entities.unique_property: THE ONLY model with validate_assignment=True (models.py:211). setattr IS validated here; model_copy still bypasses (INV-01b).

persistent_entities.consequence_of_no_writer: ledger._LIVE_CLIP_STATES (ledger.py:649) is read at ledger.py:665 as clip_live -> ALWAYS FALSE. A DEAD GUARD. Cascade protection holds entirely via _PROTECTED_POST_STATES.

persistent_entities.authoritative_owner: SPLIT — THIS IS THE ARCHITECTURE'S CENTRAL OWNERSHIP DEFECT
persistent_entities.the_split: crosspost MINTS it; Ledger.approve_post OWNS the approval gate; post.run OWNS queued->submitting; reconcile OWNS submitting/submitted/needs_reconcile; track OWNS published->analyzed. NO component owns the lifecycle of a REMOTE SUBMISSION. publish CREATES `submitting` posts it will never re-drive (RC-1); reconcile REFUSES to terminate them (RC-2). Neither owns them, so they live forever.
persistent_entities.mutation_authority_state: 21 writer sites (AST census, transitions.json). Includes 5 GENERIC/DYNAMIC writers a literal grep cannot see: cli.py:395 PostState(args.status), actions.py:870 p.state=st, run.py:357 setattr(p,f,v), reconcile.py:725 model_copy(update=upd), pipeline.py:151 model_copy(update={'state':error_state}).

persistent_entities.authoritative_owner: NONE — the entity is never minted
persistent_entities.creation_authority: ZERO. Ledger.add_render has NO CALLERS (AST). Render(...) is constructed ONLY in the two deserializers (ledger.py:458, ledger_bridge.py:44). crosspost.py:225 hardcodes render_id=None on every minted Post. LIVE LEDGER (read-only): 0 renders, 0 of 347 posts carry a render_id.
persistent_entities.consequence: led.renders is ALWAYS EMPTY. Every led.renders.get(...) returns None. All Render.path writes sit behind `if r is not None:` guards that are ALWAYS FALSE. RenderState is driverless (FIND-001, upheld across 5 cycles).
persistent_entities.forward_hazard: IF-1 — a future reframe slice that gives add_render its first caller REACTIVATES the dormant Render.path writes.

persistent_entities.note: The ONLY fully-driven auxiliary state machine in the system — every declared state has a writer.
Born `open`; NO writer AND NO reader for closed/error. models.py:488 concedes it: 'born open; this build only ever sets open'.

mutation_authority: ledger.py:562 add_imported_media — a TRUE UPSERT (last-write-wins), deliberately NOT setdefault, so a re-pull's fresher metrics win
persistent_entities.note: The ONE entity with a NATURAL key (the platform's own Graph media_id) and no lineage by construction.

mutation_authority: 10 mutators: write_integration:372, set_backend:400, add_account:438, ensure_channel:474, set_status:513, set_clip_profile:536, set_ig_user_id:561, set_persona:585, remove_account:605, link_persona:320
control_files.second_writer: THE OPERATOR, BY HAND. accounts.py:112-113 documents hand-editing as the normal channel. This is the door RC-3 comes through: set_backend normalizes+validates; the LOAD boundary does not.
read_authority: config (LAZILY — config.py reaches UP to accounts)

control_files.note: Gates learning_validated. AUTO-stamped by the first real non-degraded live metric.
Secrets go to the OS KEYRING ONLY, never plaintext .env.

control_files.authoritative_owner: fanops_account_stats / reconcile / learn_doctor / (UNKNOWN consumer — see UNK-004)

locks.primitive: SQLite BEGIN IMMEDIATE on a dedicated connection

locks.owner: fanops.ledger._file_lock (reused by accounts, personas, hashtags, meta_graph)

locks.excludes: NOTHING. Mutually invisible to L1's BEGIN IMMEDIATE.
locks.verdict: RC-4 / C4-F2a — PROVEN silent data loss AND silent media deletion. The sibling SIX LINES ABOVE (Ledger.snapshot, ledger.py:540) takes the CORRECT lock (store.lock()).

L1_L2_exclusion: NONE (COUP-01). The ledger and the control files do not exclude each other. Handled for the one case that matters: publish re-resolves the integration id at publish time and FINALIZE merges account_id with an explicit 'in-flight wins' policy.

identity_minting.rule: SHA-1, usedforsecurity=False, \x00-joined, truncated to 12 hex. Builtin hash() is BANNED (PEP 456 salts it per interpreter). ids.py:2,7.
identity_minting.consumers: every entity id in the system
identity_minting.coupling: COUP-04 — ledger._SID_RE hardcodes the FORMAT ids.py mints; changing the id length silently breaks orphan detection.

config.note: THE most-depended-on module in the system. Depth 0 (no compile-time fanops imports) yet it lazily reaches UP to accounts and meta_graph.

ownership_pathologies.asset: the lifecycle of a remote submission (Post in submitting/submitted/needs_reconcile)
ownership_pathologies.detail: publish CREATES the state and will never re-drive it; reconcile is its SOLE reader and cannot always terminate it. No component owns it. This is the root of RC-1+RC-2 and the single largest architectural hole in the system.

ONE free-text field, ~14 writers, FOUR machine semantics: a retry counter (transient_daemon_retry=n/3), a terminal marker (GAVE UP: prefix), a quarantine sentinel (unverified:), and a do-not-look-again LATCH (reconcile.py:767 `if post.error_reason: continue`). The breadcrumb that tells the operator a post is stuck IS the mechanism that stops the system looking at it again.

Normalized at ONE write boundary (accounts.set_backend) and consumed RAW by FIVE resolvers with FOUR different unknown-value behaviours. No canonical normalize-and-validate at the READ boundary. RC-3.
compress.maybe_shrink_for_cap mkdtemps it (the ONLY mkdtemp in src/fanops, AST-confirmed) and NOTHING removes it (no rmtree anywhere in the tree except transcribe's demucs cleanup). RC-10.

ownership_pathologies.class: CIRCULAR OWNERSHIP (SUBSYSTEM level — NOT an import cycle)
ownership_pathologies.detail: *** S08 selection <-> S14 learning is a cycle in the SUBSYSTEM graph (moments -> moment_hook_learning; adjust -> moments). IT IS NOT A MODULE-LEVEL IMPORT CYCLE — both modules are singleton SCCs, and the domain core is ACYCLIC at module level. *** What it DOES mean: selection and learning are MUTUALLY DEPENDENT and cannot be reasoned about, or extracted, independently. What it does NOT mean: any load-order fragility.
ownership_pathologies.intentionality: The DOMAIN feedback loop is intentional and IS the product (CERTAIN). Whether the MUTUAL-IMPORT WIRING that realizes it is intentional is UNKNOWN (UNK-C5-4) — the loop could equally be closed by the orchestrator or via a shared abstraction.

ownership_pathologies.detail: The OPERATOR is an unmodelled writer of three control files, by documented design. Every load-boundary validation gap is therefore reachable. This is not a bug; it is an ownership fact the code must be written against.
